x-enumeration-literals.name: Regulatory Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Blocks required by laws, regulations, or legal authorities.

Comment:
Includes: AML, sanctions, tax, court orders

Example: Funds are blocked due to anti-money laundering (AML) investigation.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Dispute Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Blocks related to transaction disputes or chargebacks.

Comment:
Includes: Chargeback pending, transaction disputes

Example: The amount of a disputed card transaction is blocked until the investigation is completed.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Collateral Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Blocks used to secure financial obligations or exposures.

Comment:
Includes: Loan security, margin requirements

Example: €20,000 is blocked in a deposit account to secure a credit facility.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Operational Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Blocks caused by system processes or temporary operational needs.

Comment:
Includes: Authorization holds, reconciliation, time-bound locks

Example: A hotel places a hold on €500 during a customers stay.

Funds are blocked during account maintenance or system reconciliation.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Compliance Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Blocks driven by internal policies and compliance controls.

Comment:
Includes: KYC pending, internal policy violations

Example: Funds are blocked until customer KYC information is updated and verified.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Fraud Risk Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Blocks applied to mitigate financial or fraud-related risks.

Comment:
Includes: Fraud suspicion, unusual activity

Example: An account balance is partially blocked after unusual transaction patterns are identified.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Authorization Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A temporary block placed on funds to secure a card or payment transaction before final settlement.

Comment:
Examples:
Hotel check-in deposit
Fuel station pre-authorization
Online purchase pre-approval

x-enumeration-literals.name: Reservation Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds set aside for a future planned obligation initiated by customer or system.

Comment:
Example: Reserved funds for rent payment

x-enumeration-literals.name: Pending Settlement Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds held while a transaction is authorized but not yet fully cleared or posted.

Comment:
Example: Card payment clearing delay

x-enumeration-literals.name: Minimum Balance Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds reserved to ensure a minimum required balance is maintained in an account.

Comment:
Example: Savings account minimum balance requirement

x-enumeration-literals.name: Charge Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds reserved to cover expected or scheduled banking fees and charges.

Comment:
Example: Monthly maintenance fee reservation

x-enumeration-literals.name: Sweep Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds temporarily restricted as part of a cash management or sweep arrangement between accounts.

Comment:
Example: End-of-day cash sweep to master account

x-enumeration-literals.name: Credit Protection Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds blocked to ensure credit exposure does not exceed approved limits.

Comment:
Example: Blocking available balance to maintain credit buffer

x-enumeration-literals.name: Garnishment Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds restricted due to a legal claim or court order from a third party creditor.

Comment:
Example: Wage garnishment order

x-enumeration-literals.name: Pledge Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds formally committed under a contractual pledge agreement as security for obligations.

Comment:
Example: Pledged deposit for bank guarantee issuance

x-enumeration-literals.name: Escrow Block
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Funds held in escrow until predefined conditions between parties are satisfied.

Comment:
Example: Property purchase escrow funds
